goal: Add per-customer semantic memory: a rolling profile summary, updated by an LLM when a ticket closes, and always injected into context.

rules.isolation_invariant: all customer reads use WHERE customer_id = ?

status_legend.blocked: Cannot continue until a documented blocker resolves.

features.title: customers table holds a summary column
features.user_visible_behavior: The customers table has a nullable `summary` TEXT column; db_setup.py builds it and is still safe to re-run.
features.notes: Column defaults to NULL. Existing seed inserts must still succeed.

features.title: update_customer_summary folds a closed ticket into the summary
features.user_visible_behavior: Calling update_customer_summary(customer_id, ticket, summarizer=...) persists an updated, concise summary (<= ~120 words). Accepts the no-prior-summary case.
features.notes: Takes an optional `summarizer` callable (defaults to the real Groq call + retry-on-429) so tests pass a deterministic stub and the gate needs no network. All DB access stays in memory.py. Assert STRUCTURAL properties (persisted, non-empty, length bound) — never exact wording.

features.title: close_ticket sets resolution and triggers a summary update
features.user_visible_behavior: close_ticket(ticket_id, resolution) marks the ticket Closed, stores the resolution, and folds it into the owning customer's summary. It is the only path that mutates a summary.
features.notes: Closing customer A's ticket must not touch customer B's summary (isolation). Tests inject the stub summarizer.

features.title: get_customer_context injects the summary at the top
features.user_visible_behavior: When a summary exists it appears at the top of the context block, above the raw ticket list. When absent, context still renders cleanly (no literal 'None', no crash).
features.notes: Re-runs the M0 recall + isolation tests so this change can't silently regress them.

features.title: backfill summaries from existing closed tickets
features.user_visible_behavior: A one-time, idempotent backfill folds each seed customer's CLOSED tickets into a summary. Open tickets are excluded, so no summary claims a resolution that didn't happen.
features.notes: Gate runs with the stub summarizer (deterministic, offline). Run once for real against Groq to eyeball 1001's summary — that live run is manual, not part of the gate. 1003's OPEN refund ticket must not be folded in.

features.title: surface summary in UI + trace, and lock the M1 gate
features.user_visible_behavior: The Streamlit UI shows the selected customer's current summary (read-only). verify.sh runs the full M1 suite and exits 0.
features.notes: Manual checks (record in evidence notes): summary visible in UI; one Langfuse trace shows the summary inside the assembled context. Automated: full verify.sh green.
